body:
Title: CRA Form T2038(IND) 2025 Investment Tax Credit (Individuals).

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2038-ind/t2038-ind-25e.pdf

CRA T2038(IND) 2025 calculates individual investment tax credits. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.30, 0.40, and 0.80. Part A line 1 multiplies the SR&ED qualified expenditure base by 15%, encoded as 0.15. Part A line 2 multiplies 80% of agricultural organization SR&ED contributions by 15%, encoded as 0.80 and 0.15. Part A line 3 enters partnership SR&ED ITC allocation. Part A line 4 multiplies qualified property investment by 10%, encoded as 0.10. Amount A adds amounts 1 to 4. Part B amount B multiplies METC expenditures by 15%, encoded as 0.15. Amount C multiplies CMETC expenditures by 30%, encoded as 0.30. Amount D multiplies the apprenticeship job creation credit base by 10%, encoded as 0.10. Amount E adds 20% of pre-2014 SR&ED assistance repayments and 15% of 2015-and-later repayments, encoded as 0.20 and 0.15. Amount F adds amounts B, C, D, and E. Part C recapture line 3 adds 15% of after-2013 SR&ED recapture expenditures and 20% of before-2014 SR&ED recapture expenditures.

Part D amount 4 adds amounts 2 and 3. Amount 5 adds opening balance amount 1 and amount 4. Amount G subtracts graduated rate estate adjustment amount 6 from amount 5. Amount 9 subtracts line 41000 from line 40600 and floors negative amounts at zero. Amount H subtracts line 41400 from amount 9 and floors negative amounts at zero. Amount I is the lesser of amount G or amount H. If alternative minimum tax applies, amount 13 adds amount H and line 40500, amount J subtracts T691 Part 1 line 103 from amount 13 and floors negative amounts at zero, and amount K is the lesser of amount G or amount J. Amount 15 is the ITC claim, not more than amount I or amount K whichever applies, and is entered on line 41200.

Part E amount 3 subtracts opening balance amount 2 from amount 1. Amount 6 subtracts opening balance amount 5 from maximum Part D claim amount 4 and floors negative amounts at zero. Amount L subtracts amount 6 from amount 3. Amount M adds lines 67200, 67210, and 67220 and cannot be more than amount L. Amount 9 subtracts Part D adjustment amount 6 from current-year refundable amount A. Amount 12 subtracts opening balance from amount 15. Amount 14 adds amount 12 and amount M. Amount 16 subtracts amount F from amount 14 and floors negative amounts at zero. Amount N subtracts amount 16 from amount 9. Amount 17 is the refund designation and cannot be more than amount N. Amount O multiplies amount 17 by the refundable rate 40%, encoded as 0.40, and is entered on line 45400. Part F amount 5 adds amount 2, amount 3, and amount 4. Part F amount 6 subtracts amount 5 from amount G as the carryforward to a subsequent tax year.
